title: Neuroendocrine Tumors of the Lung: Updates and Diagnostic Pitfalls.
abstract: Neuroendocrine tumors of the lung constitute approximately 20% of all primary lung tumors and include typical carcinoid, atypical carcinoid, small cell carcinoma, and large cell neuroendocrine carcinoma. Given their morphologic overlap with diverse mimics, neuroendocrine tumors of the lung can be diagnostically challenging. This review discusses the clinical, histologic, immunophenotypic, and molecular features of pulmonary neuroendocrine tumors, along with common diagnostic pitfalls and strategies for avoidance.
